content:
6 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

I need to warn you upfront: this is going to be an anticlimax.
If you came here hoping for the secret prompt, the one config file that 10x’s your output, the workflow that turns you into a one-person engineering department overnight, you’re going to be disappointed. Because when you actually look at what the developers getting real value from AI agents are doing, it’s shockingly unsexy.
They’re not vibe coding. They’re not letting the agent run wild across the whole repo. They’re doing something that looks a lot like careful, slightly boring engineering, except now there’s a very fast, very confident intern in the loop who occasionally hands them a masterpiece and occasionally hands them a disaster with the exact same facial expression.
Let me show you what the actual evidence says, because it’s more interesting than the hype and way more useful.
What the people who do this well actually do
There’s a field study I keep coming back to, where researchers observed experienced developers, all with three-plus years of professional experience, using coding agents in real work. Thirteen close observations, plus a survey of ninety-nine more. The findings are almost funny in how much they contradict…
